The Texas Rangers lost another series, this time at Detroit. The offense scored five times in the series-opening win Friday but only one run in each of the weekend losses. A team can only give the opposing pitchers so much credit. At some point, things need to change, and perhaps the Rangers will consider some changes to rejuvenate the offense. videos.title: UK | 04th May, Monday -RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 04th May, Monday -RIFT Presentation 04/05/26

🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 04/05/26

videos.title: BREAKING: DALLAS MAVERICKS HIRE MASAI UJIRI AS TEAM PRESIDENT | DLLS MAVS PODCAST
videos.summary_source_text:
BREAKING: DALLAS MAVERICKS HIRE MASAI UJIRI AS TEAM PRESIDENT | DLLS MAVS PODCAST ***THE MASAI NEWS BREAKS LIVE AT 58:42***

Late in our daily live show, news broke that the Dallas Mavericks hired Masai Ujiri as their new president of basketball operations. The DLLS Mavs crew reacts LIVE to the news, including what it could mean for building around Cooper Flagg, if (and how) it affects Jason Kidd's job, the draft and transaction cycle this summer, and Patrick Dumont's level of involvement in the decision-making moving forward. Plus, Marc Stein joins with more insight into the hire, Ujiri's past experience in the league, and much more.

Before the breaking news, we discussed Kyrie Irving. The Dallas Mavericks superstar point guard should be back for opening night next season. Will he be on the Mavs? We hope so. If he is, and if he’s feeling good, just how big of an offensive and leadership boost would he provide for a team that’s missed the playoffs two years in a row without him? The DLLS Mavs crew explores that question. We also visit with our friends from Big Brothers Big Sisters at their golf tournament fundraiser in Castle Hills.

#cooperflagg #dallasmavs #nba #pjwashington #danielgafford #klaythompson #najimarshall #jasonkidd #khrismiddleton #kyrieirving #marvinbagley #derecklively

58:42 - THE MAVS HIRING MASAI NEWS BREAKS
1:15:41 - Marc Stein joins the show diving into everything Masai related
2:00:13 - Tankathon spin time!

videos.title: Hindi | 04th May, Monday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 04th May, Monday - RIFT Presentation 04/05/26

🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 04/05/26

videos.title: Will Either Goalie Have a Short Lease In Game 2 After the 15 Goal Game 1?
videos.summary_source_text:
Will Either Goalie Have a Short Lease In Game 2 After the 15 Goal Game 1? Colorado Avalanche face goalie questions after wild 15-goal Game 1 shootout against the Minnesota Wild. Will either team make a change in net for Game 2, or simply rely on a short leash for their starters? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down postgame reactions from Jared Bednar and John Hynes, examine Scott Wedgewood and Jesper Wallstedt’s resilience, and debate whether a goaltending switch or stronger defense is key for both clubs moving forward.

The conversation also spotlights standout performances, including Trent Miner's heroics with the Colorado Eagles in the Calder Cup playoffs, and dissects the race for the Bill Masterton Trophy with Gabriel Landeskog, Jonathan Toews, and Rasmus Dahlin as finalists. Predictions for Game 2, listener insights, and the impact of defensive adjustments take center stage as the Avalanche aim to build momentum in this high-octane playoff matchup.

videos.title: Marco D’Amico on Suzuki, Goaltending & Canadiens Game 7 Victory | The Sheet
videos.summary_source_text:
Marco D’Amico on Suzuki, Goaltending & Canadiens Game 7 Victory | The Sheet Season 3 rolls on as Jeff Marek is joined by Marco D'Amico to break down the Montreal Canadiens thrilling Game 7 victory over the Tampa Bay Lightning, powered by elite goaltending and clutch playoff moments. They dive into the standout performances, including Nick Suzuki’s dominant two-way play, Alex Newhook’s gritty goal, and how Montreal found ways to beat Andrei Vasilevskiy in high-pressure situations. The guys also discuss frustration from stars like Nikita Kucherov and Brandon Hagel, plus what it all means moving forward. Then, they turn their attention to the Canadiens’ second-round matchup with the Buffalo Sabres, breaking down key storylines, matchups including Tage Thompson, and what to watch as the series gets underway.

Subscribe for more NHL breakdowns, playoff coverage, and daily hockey insight.

videos.title: Love at First Sight: How “Stuntman” Griffith Found Boxing 🥊
videos.summary_source_text:
Love at First Sight: How “Stuntman” Griffith Found Boxing 🥊 Some moments change everything.

For Ebenezer “Stuntman” Griffith, it happened the first time he stepped into a boxing gym in Louisville, Kentucky — the hometown of Muhammad Ali.

What started as curiosity instantly became purpose.

In this episode of The Weekly Cut, Griffith breaks down the moment he fell in love with the “sweet science” — not as a fight, but as an art form. Hosted by Justin Simmons and Louie Romero.

“You could see the beauty… like ballet.”

Cut by: rrheadlabs.com Some moments change everything.

OpenClaw 5.4 Beta: Faster Google Meet Voice, New Status Labels, Tool Summaries + Should You Update?

This episode covers the OpenClaw 5.4 beta update, highlighting a major speed improvement for Google Meet voice calls when agents dial in via Twilio, with smoother streaming, queue management, and interruption handling powered by Google’s Gemini voice system. It also introduces one-word progress status labels (thinking/searching/writing) across Discord, Telegram, Slack, Matrix, and Microsoft Teams, with cleaner formatted boxes and trimming for long tool runs. OpenClaw now shows summarized tool outputs by default (with an optional raw mode), adds dashboard refinements (active agent indicator, collapsible scheduled tasks, and collapsed duplicate messages), and improves startup performance by deferring heavy initialization and speeding add-on loading. The script advises caution because it’s a beta, recommends backing up first, explains how to install beta and roll back to stable, and suggests waiting if you don’t need the new features.

videos.summary_source_text:
Avs Hit Reset After Shakey Details Leads to Chaotic Game 1 Win | GS Off Ice Jesse Montaño and Meghan Angley break down one of the wildest playoff games you’ll ever see as the Colorado Avalanche take Game 1 in a 9-6 thriller over the Minnesota Wild. After a night to process the chaos, the Off Ice crew dives deeper into what really happened — from defensive breakdowns and penalty kill execution to the importance of winning Game 1 at home.

The Avs’ offense exploded for nine goals, but questions remain about defensive structure, goaltending, and whether this level of chaos can continue in the series. Plus, key insights from Jared Bednar, locker room reactions, and adjustments Colorado MUST make heading into Game 2.

videos.title: Cam Reid on the Kitchener Rangers, Being Drafted by Nashville & NHL Aspirations | Beyond the Crest
videos.summary_source_text:
Cam Reid on the Kitchener Rangers, Being Drafted by Nashville & NHL Aspirations | Beyond the Crest Jeff Marek sits down with Kitchener Rangers defenseman Cam Reid for an in-depth conversation on his hockey journey, from growing up in Elmer to becoming a key piece of the Rangers blue line. Reid reflects on his early days in the game, including playing in The Brick Tournament, his development through Power Edge Pro, and what it was like leaving home to chase the OHL dream. He shares insights on life with the Kitchener Rangers, the impact of coaches like Brad Flynn, and the competitiveness of facing top OHL talent. Reid also discusses being drafted by the Nashville Predators, learning from players like Roman Josi, and what it will take to reach the next level. Plus, stories about skating with NHLers and the mindset driving his push toward a Memorial Cup.

Subscribe for more interviews, stories, and hockey insight right here on The Sheet.

If you want deep Colorado Avalanche analysis, Val Nichushkin highlights, and full NHL playoff breakdowns after Avs vs Wild, this is the film study you need.

#nhl #hockey #avs #coloradoavalanche #avalanche #stanleycupplayoffs #nhlplayoffs #goavsgo #valnichushkin #minnesotawild #quinnhughes

videos.title: Control Starts with This Habit
videos.summary_source_text:
Control Starts with This Habit Silence can feel uncomfortable at first, especially when the instinct is to stay busy and keep moving. But over time, it builds the ability to focus and direct attention with intention. Instead of reacting to every thought, awareness becomes something that can be guided. This is where real control begins, not by doing more, but by learning to be still.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Cardiovascular Interventions, P.A. in Orlando Florida where, since 1990,  he has been repeatedly recognized in local publications as a Top Doctor performing thousands of interventional procedures in hospital and out patient settings. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease . He is also a clinical assistant professor of medicine at The Florida State University and University of Central Florida. videos.title: Your Mohawk 10-2 Is Wrong Without This Order
videos.summary_source_text:
Your Mohawk 10-2 Is Wrong Without This Order The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: Coach Prime and Colorado’s COVETED transfer who’s gaining 2027 NFL Draft buzz
videos.summary_source_text:
Coach Prime and Colorado’s COVETED transfer who’s gaining 2027 NFL Draft buzz On today’s DNVR Buffs Podcast, Jake Schwanitz and Scott Procter discuss the Colorado transfer who is earning 2027 NFL Draft buzz this summer after impressing at the Group of 6 level last year. New Colorado defensive line transfer Ezra Christensen’s draft stock has seen a steady rise since committing to Colorado this winter, the guys discuss what a successful 2026 season would look like for Christensen and an update in his quest to seek eligibility for the 2026 season. The guys also discuss the latest on Brendan Sorsby and his eligibility for 2026, a look at returning production trends over the years and in the Big 12 for 2026, the latest NFL Buff to sign with a team, graduation in Boulder over the weekend and the clips seen from the Cleveland Browns war room during the 2026 NFL Draft. The guys then discuss and draft the Big 12’s all time best wide receivers and much more.

videos.title: Dallas Stars Post Mortem Begins As Coach & Players Hold End-Of-Season Press Conferences
videos.summary_source_text:
Dallas Stars Post Mortem Begins As Coach & Players Hold End-Of-Season Press Conferences Become a DLLS Diehard and get access to premium content, our Discord channel, discounts on merch, and a free shirt! Sign up here: https://alldlls.com/join-diehard/

The offseason has officially begun for the Dallas Stars and it’s at least 2-4 weeks earlier than expected. The Stars held their end-of-season media availability this morning at Comerica Center in Frisco for head coach Glen Gulutzan and the players. (Stars GM Jim Nill will meet with the media at a later date TBD) On today’s show, we will present our 3 Stars & 3 Villains from the first round series with the Minnesota Wild. Then we will tackle our immediate takeaways from this morning’s press conferences, which lasted just over 90 minutes. If we have time, we’ll dive into the Stanley Cup Playoffs, including Game 7 of Montréal Canadiens-Tampa Bay Lightning and the wheels off Game 1 of the second round between the Colorado Avalanche and the Minnesota Wild. If we have time, we’ll also touch on the latest NHL News & Notes from around the league.

videos.title: Pressure Builds in Tampa Bay as Canadiens Advance + Round 2 Previews | DFO Rundown
videos.summary_source_text:
Pressure Builds in Tampa Bay as Canadiens Advance + Round 2 Previews | DFO Rundown Welcome to the DFO Rundown with Jason Gregor and Jeff Marek!

The NHL playoffs delivered absolute chaos this weekend, from a historic Game 7 in Montreal to a 15-goal explosion between the Colorado Avalanche and Minnesota Wild, while major offseason storylines are already starting to take shape.

Today we break down everything you need to know. The Montreal Canadiens pulled off one of the most shocking wins in recent playoff history, advancing with just nine shots on goal, raising questions about whether goaltending can carry them deeper into the postseason. Meanwhile, the Tampa Bay Lightning were eliminated again in the first round, sparking debate about whether real changes are coming or if this is simply a case of bad timing and injuries.

The Toronto Maple Leafs made a major front office move by hiring John Chayka, and the conversation quickly turns to what this means for Auston Matthews and the long-term direction of the franchise. Out west, the Edmonton Oilers, Boston Bruins, Dallas Stars, and Utah Mammoth are all heading into critical offseasons after early exits, with big questions surrounding roster construction, coaching, and potential trades.

Looking ahead, the second round is already off to a wild start. The Avalanche and Wild combined for 15 goals in Game 1 in one of the most entertaining games of the playoffs so far, but the big question remains whether that style of play is sustainable in a series that could quickly tighten up. The guys also dive into every second-round matchup, highlighting key storylines, x-factors, and what each team needs to do to advance.

It’s a full breakdown of the Stanley Cup Playoffs, plus Buy or Sell, trade speculation, and early offseason chatter that could shape the NHL landscape in the months ahead.

videos.title: Eat This Before Bed — Watch What Happens to Your Hunger, Blood Sugar & Sleep
videos.summary_source_text:
Eat This Before Bed — Watch What Happens to Your Hunger, Blood Sugar & Sleep If you’re craving something sweet at night but don’t want to spike your blood sugar or disrupt your sleep, this simple mug cake is a smarter choice. Made with low-carb, nutrient-dense ingredients like almond flour, egg, cocoa, and healthy fats, this quick dessert works with your body instead of against it by helping slow digestion, support satiety, and provide a more stable release of energy. It’s a great alternative to late-night sugar and may help reduce cravings while supporting better metabolic balance. This is not about overeating dessert, it’s about making a better choice that aligns with your body. Try it tonight and pay attention to how your hunger, energy, and sleep respond.

🥣 INGREDIENTS (Base Recipe)
3 tablespoons almond flour
(or 1 tablespoon coconut flour)
1 tablespoon unsweetened cocoa powder
1 egg
1 tablespoon melted butter or coconut oil
1 to 2 teaspoons monk fruit sweetener or stevia (adjust to taste)
¼ teaspoon baking powder
Optional: ½ teaspoon vanilla extract
🔥 INSTRUCTIONS
Add all ingredients into a mug or clear glass bowl
Mix thoroughly until smooth
Microwave for 60 to 90 seconds
Do not overcook, keep center slightly soft for best texture
🍫 OPTIONAL ADD-INS & TOPPINGS
Chopped pecans or walnuts
Dark chocolate chips (85% or sugar-free)
Fresh berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries)
Cinnamon
Nut butter (almond, peanut, or cashew)

If you don’t have a microwave, you can absolutely use the oven. Just place it in an oven safe dish at about 300 to 325 degrees and warm it for around 10 to 15 minutes until it thickens slightly and is warm, not hot. Keep an eye on it so it doesn’t overcook. You can also just let it sit in the refrigerator for 20 to 30 minutes or overnight and it will naturally thicken without any heat at all.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.title: Canadiens Advance and Leafs Hire Sundin and Chayka ft. Marco D'Amico & Nick Alberga | The Sheet
videos.summary_source_text:
Canadiens Advance and Leafs Hire Sundin and Chayka ft. Marco D'Amico & Nick Alberga | The Sheet Season 3 rolls on with Jeff Marek breaking down a thrilling night in the NHL alongside Marco D'Amico as they recap the Montreal Canadiens’ dramatic Game 7 victory over the Tampa Bay Lightning, digging into the key moments, standout performances, and what it means for a young Canadiens group finding its stride at the perfect time. The conversation then shifts to a compelling second round matchup as Montreal gets set to face the Buffalo Sabres, with a full preview of the storylines, matchups, and what to watch for in the series. Later, Nick Alberga joins Jeff to break down the latest in Toronto as the Toronto Maple Leafs officially introduce Mats Sundin and John Chayka to the front office, reacting to today’s press conference, what their arrivals signal for the organization, and how this new leadership group could shape the Leafs moving forward. Justin Simmons joins us to talk about retirement & Denver Broncos’ QB contingency plan behind Bo Nix Justin Simmons joins the DNVR Broncos Podcast to talk all things Denver Broncos!

Justin Simmons talks about his time with the Denver Broncos, his final chapter with the Atlanta Falcons, and his decision to retire as a Denver Broncos.

Plus, Justin Simmons gives his take on where the Denver Broncos can go from here. Is Bo Nix the future? Can Sean Payton be the first NFL head coach to win a Super Bowl with two different teams? How will Jaylen Waddle change the Denver Broncos’ offense?

On the DNVR Broncos Podcast, Super Bowl 50 champion Bennie Fowler, Zac Stevens and Henry Chisholm are coming to you LIVE from the DNVR Bar to break down all of the latest news in Broncos Country.

videos.title: Every Putt Given vs Auto 300 Yard Drive | Who Wins? (Peter Finch vs Ryan Ruffels)
videos.summary_source_text:
Every Putt Given vs Auto 300 Yard Drive | Who Wins? (Peter Finch vs Ryan Ruffels) This might be the most unbalanced golf match we’ve ever played…

I get every putt inside 12ft conceded — no pressure on the greens at all.
Ryan Ruffels (tour pro) starts every hole from 300 yards down the middle.

Sounds fair, right?

We put it to the test to see which advantage actually wins over 18 holes… and the result isn’t quite what you’d expect.

Let us know in the comments — who should win this?

videos.title: The Habs Are Off To Round 2 & Chayka Heads To Toronto | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
The Habs Are Off To Round 2 & Chayka Heads To Toronto | Daily Faceoff Live The Montreal Canadiens are moving on — and doing it in one of the most unbelievable Game 7 performances you’ll ever see.

In this episode of Daily Faceoff Live, we break down how the Montreal Canadiens punched their ticket to Round 2 despite generating just 10 shots on goal. Is Jakub Dobes the real deal, and can his run continue?

We also dive into what’s next for the Tampa Bay Lightning after a disappointing exit. Is a coaching change in Tampa Bay even realistic — or just wishful thinking for teams like the Leafs and Oilers?

Over in the West, the Colorado Avalanche and Minnesota Wild put on an absolute show, combining for 15 goals in a chaotic Game 1. Is this series destined to be the most unpredictable of Round 2?

Plus, we bring in Jeff Marek to discuss the biggest storyline in Toronto: the arrival of John Chayka. What do the Toronto Maple Leafs see in him, and what’s at the top of his offseason to-do list? Does it start behind the bench with Craig Berube, or is there a bigger move coming?

videos.title: Tony DeAngelo Opens & Round Two Begins | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Tony DeAngelo Opens & Round Two Begins | Morning Cuppa Hockey The Playoffs are heating up and so is the debate on Morning Cuppa Hockey. 

Former NHL Defenceman Colby Cohen and Jonny Lazarus kick things off with Tony DeAngelo of the New York Islanders joining the show LIVE - talking life on Long Island, the offseason ahead, and what's really going on with one of the NHL's most interesting franchises. 

Then Colby goes there... Again. Matvei Michkov struggles in Game 1 vs the Hurricanes. Kevin Kurz joins to break down the second round matchup between Philadelphia and Carolina.

To close it out, veteran hockey insider Gary Lawless tees up the Vegas Golden Knights vs Anaheim Ducks series - what to watch, who has the edge, and whether the Ducks Cinderalla run has legs. 

Honest. Unfiltered. The hockey conversation nobody else is having.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

• Full 18-hole course vlogs

• Matches against tour players and creators

• Some of the world’s best golf courses

• Bryson DeChambeau

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

videos.title: K64. Fattigveckan: Jimmy levde på 100 kr i en vecka och livet blev rikare
videos.summary_source_text:
K64. Fattigveckan: Jimmy levde på 100 kr i en vecka och livet blev rikare Vad händer om du lever på 100 kronor mat i veckan, sover på golvet och bara får använda internet på biblioteket? Jimmy Andersson (@FireJimmy) hörde av sig till oss efter att ha blivit inspirerad att prova att göra en fattigvecka. Budgeten för veckan var 100 kr till mat, begränsad internetdelning, endast cykel som transportmedel och begränsad tillgång till internet. Caroline har provat en lightversion av utmaningen och delar sina perspektiv på utmaningen.

Personligen blev jag inte taggad alls efter att ha lyssnat på Jimmy och Caroline :rofl: Men jag förstår andemeningen med att verkligen ifrågasätta vad som är ett rikt liv för en själv. Likt Nassim Taleb brukar prata om “via negativa” - alltså förbättring genom borttagning. Se det här avsnittet som insyn i ett experiment och dela gärna dina tankar om avsnittet i kommentarerna.

5 guldkorn från avsnittet:
▸  Hundra kronor mat för hela veckan - Jimmy levde på ärtsoppa, havregrynsgröt, spagetti och knäckebröd — allt handlat på en gång för exakt hundra kronor. Mot slutet var det så tråkigt att han inte ens åt upp allt.

▸  Caroline klarade fyra dagar - hon gav upp inte av hunger utan för att hon saknade det som faktiskt betyder något för henne: kaffe, frukt, en ordentlig dusch och sin hudvård. Det var insikten i sig — att hon nu visste vad som spelade roll.

▸  Man kan inte simulera verklig fattigdom - Jimmys viktigaste insikt: det experimentet inte kan återskapa är den psykologiska otryggheten. Han vet hur det känns på riktigt — att leva på strax under hyran i A-kassa och inte veta när det tar slut. Den rädslan går inte att kopiera med en planerad vecka.

▸  Tacksamheten efteråt är verklig - Båda beskriver samma känsla när veckan var slut: en nästan överraskande uppskattning för det vanliga. Carolines ord: “Gud vad privilegierad jag är.”

▸  Light-versionen funkar också - Caroline rekommenderar att man anpassar utmaningen efter sig själv. Välj de begränsningar som faktiskt utmanar just dig, kör i tre dagar istället för sju om det känns mer rimligt. Effekten — perspektivet — brukar komma ändå.

Vi hoppas att du gillar avsnittet!
Jan, Caroline och Jimmy

Caroline har provat en lightversion av utmaningen och delar sina perspektiv på utmaningen.

Personligen blev jag inte taggad alls efter att ha lyssnat på Jimmy och Caroline :rofl: Men jag förstår andemeningen med att verkligen ifrågasätta vad som är ett rikt liv för en själv. In this video, we break down the ultimate budget-friendly protein that delivers EVERYTHING your body needs — without draining your wallet. 

Whether you're trying to build muscle, lose fat, or just optimize your nutrition, this underrated protein source might completely change how you eat.

Stick around to find out what it is and why it outperforms even the most expensive supplements on the market.

He Went From Amateur To Pro Level Ball Striking In 20 Minutes! In this golf lesson Eric Cogorno helps a high handicap golfer fix his over-the-top swing— live on camera.

🏌️ WORK WITH ERIC COGORNO IN PERSON! Two-Day Golf Schools in Florida and Pennsylvania - Limited Spots Available!

📚 STEP-BY-STEP TRAINING PROGRAMS Eric's Complete Golf Improvement System 
👉 Start Here: https://ericcogornogolf.podia.com

🛒 RECOMMENDED GOLF EQUIPMENT & TRAINING AIDS:
• Takomo Golf - https://takomogolf.com

• theHanger Training Aid - https://www.hangergolf.com/?sca_ref=944104.HqM9GYcX2I (Code: COGORNO for 15% OFF)

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
🏌️ 0:00 The Ball-Striking Problems Holding This Golfer Back 
⚡ 0:32 How to Diagnose Your Swing Plane 
💪 2:06 Why an Under-Plane Backswing Creates a Cupped Wrist 
🔥 3:02 The Core Fix for Over-the-Top Golfers 
⛳ 4:30 "Out Over Two, Down Over Four" — The Golf Drill Explained 
✅ 6:27 Adding the Ball: How to Exaggerate the Right Way 
🏆 How Much Wrist Hinge Is Too Much In The Golf Swing? 
🚀 12:00 Combining Swing Plane and Wrist Hinge Into One Backswing Feel 
💥14:20 Through-Ball Drill: Getting the Arms, Hands, and Club High and Right 
🎯 17:44 Practice Swing vs. Real Swing — What "Feel Exaggerated, Look Perfect" Means 
⛳ 18:51 Building the Habit: How to Practice These Golf Drills Over 30 Days

In This Video, You'll Discover:
• How to instantly identify whether your club is on plane, under plane, or too far inside during your backswing 
• A golf drill to eliminate the pulls and fades that plague the high handicap golfer  
• Fix your cupped wrist at the top 
• Stop guessing at your golf swing tips and start using the "feel exaggerated, look perfect" principle to know your practice reps are actually doing the right thing 
• Build a repeatable two-part practice routine that compounds real results over weeks and months 
• Apply the same golf swing analysis approach Eric Cogorno uses in live lessons to identify the first broken link in your swing chain and fix it at the source
In this real-time lesson, Eric Cogorno works through a complete golf swing analysis with a student struggling with pulls, fades, and an over-the-top path — two of the most common issues facing any high handicap golfer. Eric diagnoses the root causes (under-plane takeaway and excessive late wrist hinge) and builds a clear, drill-based plan the student can take to the driving range immediately. This is the kind of practical, no-fluff golf instruction that turns stalled games into real improvement..

Welcome to the Mark Crossfield Golf channel, your ultimate destination for all things golf! Whether you're a beginner just learning how to swing a golf club or a seasoned golfer aiming to refine your golf swing, this channel is here to help you master every aspect of your golf swing. With a blend of expert golf instruction, golf tips, practical golf lessons and honest equipment reviews, Mark Crossfield provides you with everything you need to play your best golf.

At Mark Crossfield Golf, we know that mastering your golf swing and perfecting your golf grip are fundamental to becoming a better golfer. Our videos dive deep into the essentials of a solid golf swing, teaching you how to swing a golf club with precision and power. From golf swing basics to advanced golf swing drills and techniques, we cover it all. Learn the importance of a proper golf grip. Whether you're looking to improve your golf swing takeaway or refine your golf swing tempo, we have the expert golf instruction you need.

Our channel offers a wide range of golf lessons tailored to golfers of all skill levels. If you're new to the game, our beginner golf content will guide you through the fundamentals, including the golf grip, golf stance, and how to execute a consistent golf swing. For more experienced players, we provide advanced golf lessons on topics like ball striking and perfecting your golf swing. Each golf lesson is designed to be practical and actionable, so you can apply what you learn directly on the golf course and start seeing results.

Choosing the right equipment is crucial for any golfer. We provide thorough reviews of the best drivers and best irons to help you make informed decisions. Whether you're searching for the best drivers for beginners or looking for high-performance golf clubs that can take your game to the next level, our reviews cover all the top options. Learn about the latest golf technology and discover which equipment is best suited for your game style. Our videos also explore how to use golf launch monitors and other golf training aids to improve your performance.

Every golfer knows that small adjustments can make a big difference. Our channel offers a wealth of golf swing tips designed to help you improve every part of your golf game. Learn how to hit the driver straight, avoid common mistakes, and develop a strategy for better golf shots. We provide detailed advice on mastering your short game and putting, ensuring you're equipped with the skills needed to lower your scores. From fixing your slice to hitting longer drives, our golf swing tips are here to help you play smarter and more efficiently.

Understanding the mechanics of your golf swing is key to improvement. Our slow motion golf swing analysis breaks down each part of the swing, helping you identify areas for refinement. By performing slow motion golf swing analysis, you can make precise adjustments that lead to more consistent and powerful golf shots. This in-depth approach to golf instruction is ideal for golfers who want to understand the finer points of their golf swing and improve their overall technique.

At Mark Crossfield Golf, we believe in building a community of passionate golfers. Join us as we explore every aspect of golf, from golf swing basics to golf lessons for advanced players. We encourage you to leave comments, ask questions, and share your experiences. Our community is all about learning, growing, and enjoying the game of golf together. By engaging with our content, you'll gain access to the best golf tips, golf lessons, golf swing advice and equipment reviews available.
Don’t miss out on the latest videos designed to help you improve your golf swing. By subscribing to our channel, you’ll receive regular updates on new golf tips, golf lessons, golf swing drills, and equipment reviews. Whether you want to refine your golf swing, find the best drivers, or just enjoy quality golf instruction, Mark Crossfield Golf Coaching has everything you need to play better golf and have more fun on the golf course. At Mark Crossfield Golf, we know that mastering your golf swing and perfecting your golf grip are fundamental to becoming a better golfer. Our videos dive deep into the essentials of a solid golf swing, teaching you how to swing a golf club with precision and power. From golf swing basics to advanced golf swing drills and techniques, we cover it all. Learn the importance of a proper golf grip. Whether you're looking to improve your golf swing takeaway or refine your golf swing tempo, we have the expert golf instruction you need.

Our channel offers a wide range of golf lessons tailored to golfers of all skill levels. If you're new to the game, our beginner golf content will guide you through the fundamentals, including the golf grip, golf stance, and how to execute a consistent golf swing. For more experienced players, we provide advanced golf lessons on topics like ball striking and perfecting your golf swing. Each golf lesson is designed to be practical and actionable, so you can apply what you learn directly on the golf course and start seeing results.

Choosing the right equipment is crucial for any golfer. We provide thorough reviews of the best drivers and best irons to help you make informed decisions. Whether you're searching for the best drivers for beginners or looking for high-performance golf clubs that can take your game to the next level, our reviews cover all the top options. Learn about the latest golf technology and discover which equipment is best suited for your game style. Our videos also explore how to use golf launch monitors and other golf training aids to improve your performance.

Every golfer knows that small adjustments can make a big difference. Our channel offers a wealth of golf swing tips designed to help you improve every part of your golf game. Learn how to hit the driver straight, avoid common mistakes, and develop a strategy for better golf shots. We provide detailed advice on mastering your short game and putting, ensuring you're equipped with the skills needed to lower your scores. From fixing your slice to hitting longer drives, our golf swing tips are here to help you play smarter and more efficiently.

Understanding the mechanics of your golf swing is key to improvement. Our slow motion golf swing analysis breaks down each part of the swing, helping you identify areas for refinement. By performing slow motion golf swing analysis, you can make precise adjustments that lead to more consistent and powerful golf shots. This in-depth approach to golf instruction is ideal for golfers who want to understand the finer points of their golf swing and improve their overall technique.

At Mark Crossfield Golf, we believe in building a community of passionate golfers. Join us as we explore every aspect of golf, from golf swing basics to golf lessons for advanced players. videos.title: Kimi Wins... But Are McLaren Closer? | 2026 Miami GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Kimi Wins... But Are McLaren Closer? | 2026 Miami GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast Tom Clarkson is joined by F1 correspondent Lawrence Barretto and F1TV expert James Hinchcliffe to reflect on an action-packed Miami Grand Prix weekend. 

Kimi Antonelli made it three race wins in a row to extend his championship lead to 20 points. Was this the toughest win of Kimi’s career and what does that tell us about him? 

On the other side of the Mercedes garage, how will George Russell be feeling about the title fight after losing out to his teammate again? And what do the guys make of George’s comments about the Miami track not suiting him? 

Also on the agenda: why Lando Norris missed out on victory, Charles Leclerc’s final lap spin that cost him a podium, Red Bull’s turnaround and a very special day for Franco Colapinto. 

Plus, the guys pay tribute to former F1 driver and Paralympian Alex Zanardi, who has passed away at the age of 59. 

00:00 - Kimi's most impressive win?
12:08 - How can George hit back?
17:35 - McLaren's missed chance
24:28 - Ferrari's upgrade frustration
34:18 - Red Bull's big turnaround
39:09 - Franco feels the love
43:10 - Relief at Williams
48:25 - Remembering 'incredible human' Alex Zanardi

videos.title: LOGAN SMYTHEKOVEN! Carolina Hurricanes Take 1-0 Series Lead After 3-0 Shutout Win
videos.summary_source_text:
LOGAN SMYTHEKOVEN! Carolina Hurricanes Take 1-0 Series Lead After 3-0 Shutout Win The Carolina Hurricanes take a 1-0 series lead after winning 3-0 against the Philadelphia Flyers on Saturday night. The second line dominates again, and Frederik Andersen continues to play lights-out hockey. What stood out in the win? What should we expect for Game 2? On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the Hurricanes' Game 1 shutout win. What stood out in the game that saw the second line dominate again, Andersen being a brick wall, and a total of 68 PIMs given out? Who impressed Zach the most over the course of Game 1? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on who he thinks will be the front-runners of the Conn Smythe trophy. Does Logan Stankoven make a better case than Andersen or vice versa? Who has the edge through five games? Finally, Zach concludes the show with a brief preview of Monday night's Game 2 in Raleigh. What should fans expect, and who is Zach looking at to make an impact? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: Avalanche Win in Chaotic Game 1 Against the Minnesota Wild
videos.summary_source_text:
Avalanche Win in Chaotic Game 1 Against the Minnesota Wild Colorado Avalanche outlast Minnesota Wild in a wild 9-6 shootout—has a new playoff rivalry been born? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down an unforgettable, high-scoring Game 1, where Cale Makar overcame a first-period injury to net two crucial goals and the Avalanche set a franchise playoff record for most goals in a game. With standout offensive contributions from Nathan MacKinnon, Devon Toews, and Martin Nečas, Colorado’s firepower was on full display—but at what defensive cost?

The hosts spotlight defensive lapses, questions in goal for both squads, and Minnesota's impressive ability to erase a 3-0 deficit and briefly take the lead. Listeners get analysis on the Avalanche’s need for tighter defensive play, key moments like the devastating shorthanded goal, and the adjustments Jared Bednar’s squad must make for Game 2. Was this just a playoff anomaly, or is another goal-filled battle ahead? Don’t miss this fast-paced playoff breakdown.

videos.title: 3.5: Dobes leads Montreal to Game 7 victory with STELLAR performance 🇨🇿⭐
videos.summary_source_text:
3.5: Dobes leads Montreal to Game 7 victory with STELLAR performance 🇨🇿⭐ Jakub Dobes, just wow! The young Czech goaltender stops 28 of 29 shots the Lightning send his way to lead his Canadiens to a 2-1 victory in Game 7, sending Montreal to face the Sabres in the second round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs! Dobes becomes the 4th rookie goalie since 2016 to win a Game 7, joining Matt Murray, Jordan Binnington and Akira Schmid. Dobes' incredible performance earns him both 1st star of the game and Top European Player of the Night honors!

OpenClaw 5.3 Beta: Reliability Fixes, New File Transfer Plugin, and Should You Update?

OpenClaw 5.3 beta is presented as a reliability-focused update addressing recent issues like crashes, broken plugins, gateway problems, and undelivered messages. It aims to fix plugin install/update/uninstall failures, stale manifests, missing package directories, and improve Claw Hub fallback error handling. New features and fixes include a file transfer plugin (16MB limit, path-allowlist security), improved Discord typing indicators and status reaction lifecycle, Telegram topic reply delivery fixes and media group timing controls, WhatsApp channel/newsletter messaging plus accurate group-delivery confirmation, and Slack Block Kit trimming to prevent message failures. Startup is faster via deferred loading, a new cross-channel progress streaming mode and /steer command are added, macOS update/doctor automation is improved, memory/recall stability is enhanced, and security tightens around secrets and plugin integrity. The script advises not rushing to update if stable, to back up first, and to test or wait for reports in Discord.

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 4 | Cadillac Championship | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 4 | Cadillac Championship | 2026 Check out all the best shots from Round 4 of the 2026 Cadillac Championship, featuring Cameron Young, Ben Griffin, Scottie Scheffler and Rickie Fowler, among others.

The PGA TOUR heads back to the iconic Trump National Doral for the Cadillac Championship, marking the return of the famed “Blue Monster” for the first time since 2016. As the first of back-to-back Signature Events leading into the season’s second major, the field is stacked, led by world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler, who arrives in strong form after a playoff loss at the RBC Heritage and consecutive runner-up finishes. There’s plenty of firepower behind him. Collin Morikawa continues a red-hot run of results, while Cameron Young brings elite form highlighted by a win at THE PLAYERS Championship. Meanwhile, Chris Gotterup remains one of the standout performers of 2026 with multiple victories already this season. A major storyline comes from Alex Fitzpatrick, who earned entry into this Signature Event run after teaming with brother Matt Fitzpatrick to win the Zurich Classic of New Orleans. Past success at Doral also returns to the spotlight, with Adam Scott, the 2016 winner back on the Blue Monster, joined by former champion Justin Rose. With FedExCup points, Signature Event status and major momentum all on the line, the Blue Monster once again presents one of the TOUR’s toughest and most iconic tests.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped COSMO Then Mysteriously Pulled It
videos.summary_source_text:
Google Just Dropped COSMO Then Mysteriously Pulled It Google quietly dropped a mysterious new AI app called COSMO, then pulled it within hours. The app looked like an experimental Android assistant that could run with Gemini Nano, connect to remote AI, and potentially interact with what is happening on your screen. At the same time, Gemini may be getting a new Omni video model, DeepMind is testing an AI co-clinician, OpenAI updated Codex with animated pets and workflow tools, Anthropic is testing Claude Jupiter, and Mistral’s new model is getting criticized.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@science.revolution

🧠 What You’ll See
0:00 Intro
0:30 Google’s mysterious COSMO AI app appearing and disappearing within hours
SOURCE: https://9to5google.com/2026/05/01/google-cosmo-assistant/
2:09 COSMO Features & Pull
2:33 Gemini’s possible new Omni video model leak before Google I/O
SOURCE: https://www.testingcatalog.com/google-is-testing-new-omni-model-for-video-generation-ahead-of-i-o/
4:09 Google DeepMind testing an AI co-clinician for doctors and telemedicine
SOURCE: https://deepmind.google/blog/ai-co-clinician/
5:20 AI Telemedicine Tests — "The most futuristic part was telemedicine."
6:24 OpenAI adding animated pets and workflow features to Codex
SOURCE: https://www.engadget.com/2162796/openai-introduces-ai-generated-pets-for-its-codex-app/
8:11 Anthropic red-teaming a new Claude Jupiter build before its event
SOURCE: https://www.testingcatalog.com/anthropic-tests-jupiter-v1-p-before-potential-launch-on-may-6/
9:13 Mistral Medium three point five launching with agent tools and backlash
SOURCE: https://mistral.ai/news/vibe-remote-agents-mistral-medium-3-5/

🚨 Why It Matters
Google may be testing a much deeper phone assistant that can understand your device, switch between local and cloud AI, and work across apps. COSMO disappearing so fast makes it feel like something went public too early, while Gemini Omni, OpenAI Codex, Claude Jupiter, and Mistral show how fast the AI race is moving.

videos.title: Jack Pridham Interview: Michigan Goal, OHL Path & Chicago Blackhawks Future
videos.summary_source_text:
Jack Pridham Interview: Michigan Goal, OHL Path & Chicago Blackhawks Future Jeff Marek sits down with Jack Pridham, a top OHL prospect and Chicago Blackhawks draft pick, for an in-depth interview on his development path, NHL future, and rise with the Kitchener Rangers.

In this NHL prospect interview, Pridham breaks down his journey through junior hockey, including his time at St. Andrew’s College, playing alongside elite prospects, and how that experience prepared him for the OHL and the NHL Draft. He also shares insight into developing under head coach Jussi Ahokas, the impact of Kitchener’s upgraded facilities, and what it takes to succeed at the next level.

The conversation also covers his viral Michigan goal, his scoring mentality inspired by Alexander Ovechkin, and his approach to shooting, confidence, and offensive creativity. Pridham discusses life as a Blackhawks prospect, playoff mindset in the OHL, and the process of chasing an NHL career one day at a time.

Plus, he reflects on growing up in a hockey family with his father, Brandon Pridham of the Toronto Maple Leafs, and how that environment shaped his understanding of the game.

If you’re looking for NHL prospect interviews, OHL highlights, and insight into future Chicago Blackhawks players, this conversation is a must-watch.

#JackPridham #OHL #ChicagoBlackhawks #NHLProspects #HockeyInterview #JuniorHockey #KitchenerRangers #NHLDraft #HockeyDevelopment #DailyFaceoff

How will The Florida Panthers attempt at moving forward and making their team more competitive in 2026-27? How can they bounce back and move back into a playoffs spot after not making the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Playoffs. I’m 60 — These 7 Health Mistakes Cost Me 30 Years I’m 60 years old and it took me over 30 years to learn what I’m about to share with you.

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

These are the seven biggest health mistakes I made, and I’m sharing them so you don’t have to go through years of fatigue, pain, and frustration like I did.

These mistakes cost me sleep, energy, joints, digestion, and clarity, and most of them were completely avoidable.

I hope you learn from my mistakes instead of repeating them. These lessons can prevent years of misery, confusion, and wasted effort.

videos.title: Do This for 30 Seconds After Sitting — Feel the Difference Instantly
videos.summary_source_text:
Do This for 30 Seconds After Sitting — Feel the Difference Instantly Hold this simple position for just 30 seconds and notice how your body feels when you stand up. This easy wall-supported movement helps activate the glutes, legs, core, and postural muscles while improving circulation, oxygen delivery, and the brain-body connection. When your muscles contract, they help pump blood back toward the heart, support better posture, and send powerful signals to your nervous system through proprioception. If you sit for long periods, your body can start to feel heavy, tight, compressed, and sluggish because certain muscles are not firing the way they should. This quick technique helps wake those muscles back up, support your hips and lower back, improve body awareness, and make you feel more upright and responsive. Try it a few times throughout the day, especially after sitting, and pay attention to how much lighter and more connected your body feels when you stand up.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

Sir Bob Charles | Reflecting on His Open Win and Remarkable Career SUBSCRIBE -  https://bit.ly/RandaGolfSubscribe

Sir Bob Charles, the Champion Golfer of the Year in 1963, reflects on his historic Open victory at Royal Lytham & St Annes, where he became the first left-handed player to win a major championship.
 
In this interview, Sir Bob Charles looks back on his play-off win, his legendary putting, and a remarkable career. 
 
With The Open set to return to Royal Lytham & St Annes Golf Club in 2028, we revisit an iconic moment in the venue’s history.

videos.title: Unique ruling as leader Cam Young calls penalty on himself | Cadillac Championship | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
Unique ruling as leader Cam Young calls penalty on himself | Cadillac Championship | 2026 In the final round of the 2026 Cadillac Championship, Cameron Young brings over an official before hitting his second shot at the par-4 second hole. Young deems the ball moved at address, leading to a one-stroke penalty. After the penalty taken, Young is able to save par and stay 15-under par at the time.

The PGA TOUR heads back to the iconic Trump National Doral for the Cadillac Championship, marking the return of the famed “Blue Monster” for the first time since 2016. As the first of back-to-back Signature Events leading into the season’s second major, the field is stacked, led by world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler, who arrives in strong form after a playoff loss at the RBC Heritage and consecutive runner-up finishes. There’s plenty of firepower behind him. Collin Morikawa continues a red-hot run of results, while Cameron Young brings elite form highlighted by a win at THE PLAYERS Championship. Meanwhile, Chris Gotterup remains one of the standout performers of 2026 with multiple victories already this season. A major storyline comes from Alex Fitzpatrick, who earned entry into this Signature Event run after teaming with brother Matt Fitzpatrick to win the Zurich Classic of New Orleans. Past success at Doral also returns to the spotlight, with Adam Scott, the 2016 winner back on the Blue Monster, joined by former champion Justin Rose. With FedExCup points, Signature Event status and major momentum all on the line, the Blue Monster once again presents one of the TOUR’s toughest and most iconic tests.

OpenAI is building a secret device with Jony Ive — but almost everything 
you've seen on TikTok and Twitter about it is FAKE. I spent weeks digging 
through court filings, Reuters reports, and OpenAI's own job listings to 
separate the real story from the clickbait. What's actually being built 
is weirder than the rumors.

In this video, I break down exactly what OpenAI is making, when it's 
really shipping, and the simple 3-bucket filter you can use to spot a 
fake AI rumor in 10 seconds.

videos.title: This £200 Device Could Change Golf FOREVER
videos.summary_source_text:
This £200 Device Could Change Golf FOREVER Rick puts the Shot Scope LM1 launch monitor to the test on the course and range, breaking down its accuracy, ease of use, and whether it really delivers for the price. From club speed to carry distance, see how it performs in real-world conditions and in the studio. We find out if this compact device is a game-changer or just another golf gadget.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: The Rasmus Dahlin Mechanic Even Cale Makar Can't Replicate
videos.summary_source_text:
The Rasmus Dahlin Mechanic Even Cale Makar Can't Replicate The Rasmus Dahlin Mechanic Even Cale Makar Can't Replicate

This week’s free lesson on YouTube we look at something Rasmus Dahlin does that even Cale Makar and Quinn Hughes don't really use the same way. Makar is famous for his 10-and-2’s, where his hips rotate open and he releases off the inside hip almost instantly. That's a quick-release mechanic, and it's incredible at what it does.

But Dahlin is doing something different at the blue line when he is quarterbacking the powerplay. He gets into that same internally rotated position, with his toes turned inwards and the puck cocked back behind him, and instead of releasing, he holds it. He holds it long enough for F1 to turn his toecaps and lose his angle on his push down the wall. Dahlin's stance forces the penaltykiller out of position. The reason most players never use this position is that it feels fragile. It looks like you have no power, no balance, and nowhere to go. But that's exactly the illusion, because your body is actually coiled, the puck is loaded behind you, and your skates are pointed in the direction you're about to attack. The defender sees the loaded puck and the rotated hips and assumes you're about to make a play to one side, when in reality your line of travel is the opposite direction. 

This is why we lean so hard on shared language at Train 2.0. The moment you can call something "internal hip rotation" and pair it with what it looks like in a Dahlin clip, you can actually access the movement. Without the language, the mechanic stays invisible to you, even when you're watching it happen ten times a game. 

If you want to start working on this and the rest of the foundation, the free Downhill Skating training is the right place to begin. It's the same system we use to compare your video to elite NHLers and coach you into their mechanics.

1:45 — Dahlin in action: getting the defender to bite.

videos.title: I Fixed Your “Love Handles” Problem (NO, SERIOUSLY!)
videos.summary_source_text:
I Fixed Your “Love Handles” Problem (NO, SERIOUSLY!) Love handles won’t disappear from side bends, crunches, or wishful thinking. If you want to lose love handles and finally reveal a leaner, more sculpted waistline, this video breaks down the 5 steps you need to start getting rid of stubborn fat around the waist and building a better looking midsection.

The first step, as always, starts with nutrition. There are no shortcuts that allow you to avoid lowering your overall body fat if your goal is to see sharper obliques, a tighter waist, and a more ripped core. The good news is, this does not have to be overly complicated. I’m going to give you a simple starting point you can use right away to begin dropping body fat and shrinking the love handles.

Incinerate the Love Handles Here - http://athleanx.com/x/no-more-love-handles
Subscribe to this channel here - http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

First, stand in front of the mirror and see if you can make out the top row of your abs. They don’t need to be deeply carved or perfectly visible. If you can see even a faint outline of that top row, multiply your bodyweight by 15 to estimate your target daily calorie intake. If you cannot see them at all, multiply your bodyweight by 12 to create a slightly more aggressive calorie starting point.

Stay consistent with that calorie target for the next two weeks and track whether your weight is moving in the right direction. If it is not, reduce your intake by another 200 calories per day or so, with 500 calories being the most aggressive adjustment I would recommend, then reassess again. Once the scale and your body fat start trending down, you know you have found the right starting point and can continue working toward a leaner waist.

The second step is using activity to support your calorie deficit. This is where you increase your total daily calorie burn without forcing all of the progress to come from cutting more food. For most people, walking is the smartest and most sustainable form of activity for improving the waistline. It is easy to recover from, low impact, and something you can do more consistently, which makes it more powerful over the course of a full week.

Running can be too demanding on the joints for many people, especially those carrying extra body fat, and it often becomes less sustainable because of the higher effort and recovery cost required to keep doing it consistently.

The third step is one most people completely overlook. Instead of only thinking about ab exercises or exercises for the love handles, think about the visual impact of building broader shoulders and a wider back. When your delts and lats are more developed, your waist instantly appears narrower by comparison. This is where exercises like lateral raises for the middle delts and pulldowns or straight arm pushdowns for the lats become important.

Choosing the right exercises matters here, and I show you the specific ones that are going to help create that more tapered, athletic look.

Next, we can finally turn the attention to the abs themselves. The key is not just doing more ab exercises, but choosing the right ones. Top down ab movements, where your shoulders move toward your pelvis, are often easier to perform correctly and can help develop the upper abs that you are most likely to see first as your body fat starts to drop.

Remember, men tend to lose body fat in a top down pattern. This means you will usually see your abs before the love handles fully disappear. Training the abs along the way helps keep you motivated because you can start seeing progress before the last stubborn fat around the waist is gone.

Finally, you need to train the obliques. Do not avoid oblique exercises because you are afraid they will give you a blocky waist. That is one of the biggest mistakes people make. A well developed set of obliques is part of what makes the waist look athletic, sharp, and complete once the body fat comes off. The key is using the right rotational ab exercises, like the ones shown in this video, so the waist looks tighter and more defined.

Use this simple 5 step approach if you want to burn fat, lose love handles, and build a more sculpted waistline for good. When you are ready for a complete workout program and meal plan that removes all of the guesswork, head to athleanx.com and get the ATHLEAN-X Training System.

For more videos on how to lose love handles, get rid of stubborn fat around the waist, build ripped abs, and reveal a leaner midsection, make sure to subscribe to our channel through the link above and turn on your notifications so you never miss a new video. videos.title: Did Google Just Sell Out?!
videos.summary_source_text:
Did Google Just Sell Out?! Is Google selling out?

Google just signed a massive deal with the Pentagon to use its AI for classified information. This directly contradicts the ethical boundaries they set when they bought DeepMind back in 2014, and over 600 of their own employees are actively begging them not to do it.

But with the government holding the keys to the future of AI legislation and contracts, my take is that Google was kind of in a no-win situation. 

What do you think? Should they have held the line like Anthropic, or is this the right business move?

videos.title: Round 4 Highlights | 2026 Turkish Airlines Open
videos.summary_source_text:
Round 4 Highlights | 2026 Turkish Airlines Open Highlights from the final round of the 2026 Turkish Airlines Open, where the battle for the title was decided on the final stretch. 

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

There's plenty at stake for DP World Tour members as the Asian Swing reaches its climax this week at the Turkish Airlines Open. The eyes of the golfing world turn to Antalya, with Belek's National Golf Club, located within Regnum Hotels, taking on host venue duties for the first time for Türkiye's national open. Players representing 32 countries are set to tee it up, reinforcing the globality of the DP World Tour and the opportunities it provides. No fewer than seven winners so far this season are among the 156-player field, including the returning Jayden Schaper from South Africa, who at third is the highest ranked player in the Race to Dubai standings following his back-to-back wins at the tail end of 2025. While relatively young, the Turkish Airlines Open has played a significant role in the expansion of the DP World Tour’s international footprint. From its inception in 2013, when Tiger Woods made an appearance, the event quickly became a popular stop for players.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

videos.title: A simple drill for better and longer drives...
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
A simple drill for better and longer drives... This simple drill that helps us all to understand and feel the best low point for a driver swing. #golfgear #golfswing #golf 

0:00 Intro to a simple drill for better drives. 

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating.

videos.title: MY NEW in the Bag IS WILD - Tour Ready
videos.summary_source_text:
MY NEW in the Bag IS WILD - Tour Ready If you told me a few years ago that my golf bag would look like this, I would have laughed.

But things are changing. My swing is evolving, my body is getting stronger, and my ball speeds are climbing. To bridge the gaps in my game and prepare for my goal of competing in a Senior Tour event, I headed to the Srixon Performance Centre to work with their top tour fitters.

The result? A setup that might shock you.

In this video, I’m walking you through my entire 2026 In The Bag (WITB). We’re talking about why I’ve officially ditched my hybrids for utility irons, why I’m choosing "Game Improvement" irons over tour blades, and the driver setup that is giving me 157+ MPH ball speeds.

Whether you’re a gear head or just looking to fix the gaps in your own bag, there’s a lesson here: stop playing the clubs you think you should play, and start playing the ones that actually help you score.

In this video, you’ll see:

The "Ego" Irons: Why the Srixon ZXi Game Improvement irons are staying in the bag.
The Hybrid Killer: Why the ZXU 2-iron is more reliable for high-speed players.
Driver Tech: The truth about shaft weight (50g vs 60g) and stability.
The Putter "Ting": My surprising switch to a counterbalance mallet.

Are you a Hybrid or a Utility Iron player? Let me know in the comments!


FREE Golf Swing 101 Class here: https://www.markcrossfieldgolf.com/how-to-play-golf-101

NOW AVAILABLE ONLINE LESSONS WITH MARK CROSSFIELD, check out his packages here: https://skillest.com/coach/markcrossfield

videos.title: CROSSOVER: The Colorado Avalanche will take on the Minnesota Wild in Round 2 | Locked On Avalanche
videos.summary_source_text:
CROSSOVER: The Colorado Avalanche will take on the Minnesota Wild in Round 2 | Locked On Avalanche The Minnesota Wild and Colorado Avalanche clash in a high-stakes Round 2 showdown, reigniting playoff memories from 2014. Will the Avalanche’s high-octane five-on-five play overpower a resilient Wild lineup hungry for their deepest run in over a decade?

Seth Toupal of Locked on Wild and Zach Pearson break down key battles between stars like Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Kirill Kaprizov, and Quinn Hughes. Spotlight shines on X-factors including Scott Wedgewood’s red-hot goaltending and Brock Faber’s defensive dominance. Power play and penalty kill units, the center depth matchup, and the leadership of captains Gabriel Landeskog and Jared Spurgeon all come under scrutiny. Can Joel Eriksson Ek and Ryan Hartman keep pace? Will special teams or goaltending tip the series? Heated predictions and lingering heartbreak from past series set the stage for an electric Western Conference duel.

CROSSOVER: The Colorado Avalanche will take on the Minnesota Wild in Round 2 | Locked On Avalanche

videos.title: 2.5: Andersen stuns Flyers with 19-save shutout 🇩🇰⭐
videos.summary_source_text:
2.5: Andersen stuns Flyers with 19-save shutout 🇩🇰⭐ Frederik Andersen improves to 5-0 in the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs and cements his place in Hurricanes' franchise history with this DOMINANT 19-save shutout against the Philadelphia Flyers in Game 1! The Danish goalie earns his 24th playoff win with Carolina, passing Cam Ward for the most in franchise history - he also notches his 4th career playoff shutout, tying Ward for the most in franchise history. Additionally, Andersen ties Kevin Weekes for the most consecutive games allowing two or less goals to start a postseason in Hurricanes/Whalers history, with 5 such consecutive games. Andersen's outstanding performance in net earns him tonight's Top European Player of the Night honors!

videos.title: This AI Is Scarier Than AGI, ASI and Terminator
videos.summary_source_text:
This AI Is Scarier Than AGI, ASI and Terminator Scientists are warning that the next big AI threat may not look like AGI, ASI, or the Terminator. It may look like AI agents that copy, adapt, compete, and evolve online. This new idea is called evolvable AI, or eAI, and the scary part is simple: AI may become dangerous without becoming evil, conscious, or superintelligent. It may just become better at surviving, spreading, and avoiding control.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com

Scientists warning about evolvable AI and digital evolution risks
SOURCE: https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.2527700123
AI systems evolving like organisms and creating new risks
SOURCE: https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1126192
Why AI may evolve, replicate, and compete like digital life
SOURCE: https://www.unsw.edu.au/newsroom/news/2026/05/evolvable-ai-are-we-on-the-brink-of-the-next-major-evolutionary-transition

🚨 Why It Matters
AI may become dangerous without hating humans or planning anything. If AI agents start copying, adapting, and competing for resources, the strongest versions may survive and spread. That could turn the internet into a digital jungle where humans slowly lose control.

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 3 | Cadillac Championship | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 3 | Cadillac Championship | 2026 Check out all the best shots from Round 3 of the 2026 Cadillac Championship, featuring Cameron Young, Scottie Scheffler, Ben Griffin and Jordan Spieth, among others.

The PGA TOUR heads back to the iconic Trump National Doral for the Cadillac Championship, marking the return of the famed “Blue Monster” for the first time since 2016. As the first of back-to-back Signature Events leading into the season’s second major, the field is stacked, led by world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler, who arrives in strong form after a playoff loss at the RBC Heritage and consecutive runner-up finishes. There’s plenty of firepower behind him. Collin Morikawa continues a red-hot run of results, while Cameron Young brings elite form highlighted by a win at THE PLAYERS Championship. Meanwhile, Chris Gotterup remains one of the standout performers of 2026 with multiple victories already this season. A major storyline comes from Alex Fitzpatrick, who earned entry into this Signature Event run after teaming with brother Matt Fitzpatrick to win the Zurich Classic of New Orleans. Past success at Doral also returns to the spotlight, with Adam Scott, the 2016 winner back on the Blue Monster, joined by former champion Justin Rose. With FedExCup points, Signature Event status and major momentum all on the line, the Blue Monster once again presents one of the TOUR’s toughest and most iconic tests.

The PGA TOUR heads back to the iconic Trump National Doral for the Cadillac Championship, marking the return of the famed “Blue Monster” for the first time since 2016. As the first of back-to-back Signature Events leading into the season’s second major, the field is stacked, led by world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler, who arrives in strong form after a playoff loss at the RBC Heritage and consecutive runner-up finishes. There’s plenty of firepower behind him. Collin Morikawa continues a red-hot run of results, while Cameron Young brings elite form highlighted by a win at THE PLAYERS Championship. Meanwhile, Chris Gotterup remains one of the standout performers of 2026 with multiple victories already this season. A major storyline comes from Alex Fitzpatrick, who earned entry into this Signature Event run after teaming with brother Matt Fitzpatrick to win the Zurich Classic of New Orleans. Past success at Doral also returns to the spotlight, with Adam Scott, the 2016 winner back on the Blue Monster, joined by former champion Justin Rose. With FedExCup points, Signature Event status and major momentum all on the line, the Blue Monster once again presents one of the TOUR’s toughest and most iconic tests.
